question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user inject an arbitrarily small value (e.g., '1ns') into HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL before container startup, causing cfg.interval to be set to near-zero, so the ticker in main.go fires at maximum rate and floods the consensus network with CryptoTransfer transactions, partitioning legitimate traffic outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to a very large integer (e.g., 2147483647) so that cfg.maxRetries causes submitWithRetry() to loop for an unbounded number of attempts per tick, exhausting goroutine and socket resources and partitioning the pinger from the network outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF to '0s' or a negative duration string, causing toDuration() to return 1s default but the validation branch at cfg.baseBackoff <= 0 to silently reset it, so the effective backoff is always 1s regardless of attempt count, removing exponential back-pressure and causing a retry storm that partitions the network?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toDuration()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a malformed duration string (e.g., '999999999h') to HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL that parses successfully as a valid Go duration but overflows time.Duration arithmetic in the ticker, causing the ticker to fire at an unintended rate and inducing a network partition outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_TIMEOUT to an extremely large value (e.g., '87600h'), causing cfg.mirrorNodeClientTimeout to be set to 10 years, so every HTTP connection in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() holds open indefinitely, exhausting file descriptors and partitioning the pinger from the mirror node outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES to a very large integer, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to retry mirror node HTTP requests for an unbounded number of attempts, holding the HTTP client open and exhausting ephemeral ports, partitioning the pinger from the mirror node outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'other' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to an attacker-controlled URL, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to fetch a crafted /api/v1/network/nodes response that injects malicious consensus node IPs, redirecting all pinger traffic to attacker-controlled endpoints and partitioning the pinger from the real network?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to an internal RFC-1918 address (e.g., 'http://169.254.169.254') when HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK=other, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to perform an SSRF request to cloud metadata endpoints, leaking credentials that allow the attacker to further partition the network outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to a negative value (e.g., '-1'), bypassing the cfg.amountTinybar == 0 check since -1 != 0, causing AddHbarTransfer() in transfer.go to submit a negative-amount transfer that triggers unexpected consensus node behavior and partitions the pinger from the network outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to the maximum int64 value (9223372036854775807), causing AddHbarTransfer() to attempt a transfer of ~92 billion HBAR, which the consensus node rejects with an error on every attempt, causing all retries to exhaust and the pinger to continuously fail, simulating a network partition outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: envOr()] Can an unprivileged external user inject whitespace-only values (e.g., '   ') into HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK via environment, causing envOr() to return the default 'testnet' after TrimSpace, but cfg.network = strings.TrimSpace(cfg.network) in validation still passes, so the pinger silently connects to testnet instead of the intended network, causing a logical network partition outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_BASE_BACKOFF to '0s', causing toDuration() to return 1s default, but the validation at cfg.mirrorNodeClientBaseBackoff <= 0 resets it to 500ms, so the effective backoff is always 500ms regardless of attempt count, removing exponential back-pressure on mirror node retries and causing a request storm that partitions the mirror node outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to '1ns' (minimum valid Go duration), causing time.NewTicker(cfg.interval) in main.go to fire at nanosecond frequency, generating millions of CryptoTransfer transactions per second that overwhelm the consensus network and cause a network partition outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to an empty string after TrimSpace, causing cfg.network == '' to fall through to the default case in newClient() and return an 'unknown network' error, preventing the pinger from ever connecting and simulating a permanent network partition outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL with a path traversal component (e.g., 'http://mirror-rest:5551/../../../etc'), causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to construct a malformed URL that bypasses network routing rules and reaches unintended internal endpoints, partitioning the pinger from the legitimate mirror node outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_TIMEOUT to '1ns', causing the HTTP client in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to time out on every request immediately, and with a large mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries value, cause a tight retry loop that exhausts CPU and network resources, partitioning the pinger from the mirror node outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toDuration()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a duration string that parses to exactly 0 (e.g., '0s') for HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF, causing toDuration() to return 0 which triggers the cfg.baseBackoff <= 0 reset to 1s, but if the validation is bypassed via flag override, backoff() computes base * 1<<(attempt-1) = 0 for all attempts, causing a tight retry loop that partitions the network outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL pointing to a DNS name that resolves to 127.0.0.1 (DNS rebinding), causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to connect to the pinger's own localhost, returning a crafted node list that redirects all consensus traffic to loopback and partitions the pinger from the real network outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to 0 and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to '1ns', causing submitWithRetry() to make exactly one attempt per nanosecond tick with no retry, flooding the consensus network with single-attempt transactions at maximum rate and partitioning legitimate traffic outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY to the hardcoded default key '302e020100300506032b65700422042091132178e72057a1d7528025956fe39b0b847f200ab59b2fdd367017f3087137' (which is the default in loadConfig()), causing the pinger to use a well-known public key that any attacker can use to sign conflicting transactions, partitioning the operator account from the network outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID to '0.0.2' (the default genesis account), causing the pinger to attempt transfers from the treasury account, which may be rate-limited or blocked by consensus nodes, causing all transfer attempts to fail and simulating a network partition outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to '0.0.0' (the zero account), causing AddHbarTransfer() to target an invalid account that consensus nodes reject, causing all transfer attempts to fail with INVALID_ACCOUNT_ID and simulating a network partition outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to a very large duration (e.g., '8760h'), causing the ticker to fire only once per year, making the liveness heartbeat at /tmp/alive go stale after 2 minutes (per healthcheck logic), causing Kubernetes to kill the pod and simulate a network partition outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES to 0 and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'other', causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to make exactly one attempt to fetch the node list, and if the mirror node is temporarily unavailable, the pinger fails to initialize and never connects to the network, simulating a permanent network partition outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL with an extremely long path (e.g., 65535 characters), causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to construct a URL that exceeds HTTP server limits, resulting in a 414 URI Too Long error on every attempt and partitioning the pinger from the mirror node outside design parameters?
